claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you're asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have two children I'm asking about - Alex, who's 13, and Blake, who's 15.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. You have two children - Alex (13) and Blake (15). Since both are under 16, they can potentially qualify for Child Benefit.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility. Let me start with the children's circumstances:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently working in paid employment for 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work. They're both still at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know - both children are at school and not working full-time.

Now I need to ask about your relationship with the children:

**Are you the parent of Alex and Blake, or do you have parental responsibility for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm their parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about living arrangements:

**Do Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me at home, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake doesn't live with you, I need to understand that situation better.

**Can you tell me where Blake lives? Does Blake live with another parent, another family member, or in some other arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is in care at the moment - they've been there for about a month or so.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. I understand Blake has been in care for about a month.

**Are you still contributing to Blake's upkeep or maintenance costs while they are in care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute - I help out with things they need.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. The rules state that you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in care for up to 8 weeks if you contribute to their upkeep. Since Blake has been in care for about a month (approximately 4 weeks), you may still be eligible for Blake during this period.

However, I need to clarify:

**Has Blake been continuously in care for this entire period, or have they come home to stay with you for at least 2 consecutive nights per week or spent at least 24 hours per week at home with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they've been at the care home full-time - they haven't been coming home to stay with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake has been in care full-time for about a month without coming home regularly, and you've mentioned you contribute to their upkeep, the 8-week time limit is important here.

Now let me ask about your own circumstances:

**Are you currently living or working in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your immigration status:

**Are you a British citizen, or if not, do you have the right to live and work in the UK (such as settled status, pre-settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or refugee status)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. And for Blake, no one else is claiming it either.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit based on what you've told me.

Let me review the situation for each child:

**For Alex (age 13):**
- Alex is under 16
- Not working full-time (under 24 hours per week)
- Lives with you
- You are their parent
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**For Blake (age 15):**
- Blake is under 16
- Not working full-time
- Has been in care full-time for about a month (approximately 4 weeks)
- You contribute to their upkeep
- You are their parent
- You are a British citizen living in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake

Based on the UK Child Benefit rules:

✅ **You ARE eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.** Alex meets all the qualifying criteria - they are under 16, live with you, and you have parental responsibility.

✅ **You ARE currently eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.** Under the rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in care for up to 8 weeks if you contribute to their upkeep. Since Blake has been in care for approximately 4 weeks, you are still within the 8-week window.

**Important note about Blake:** After 8 weeks of continuous care (without Blake coming home to stay with you for at least 2 consecutive nights per week or spending at least 24 hours per week at home), your eligibility for Child Benefit for Blake will end. You should keep track of how long Blake has been in care and be aware that if they remain in care beyond 8 weeks without regular home visits meeting those criteria, you'll need to inform the Child Benefit Office as you would no longer be eligible for Blake's benefit.

Would you like information about how to claim, or do you have any questions about your eligibility?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, that's really helpful, thank you. I understand now - I'm eligible for both of them at the moment, but I need to keep track of how long Blake has been in care. I appreciate you explaining it all to me.
